zh.Eresby, Spilsby, Lincolnshire, England: 英國英格蘭林可爾史爾斯皮爾斯比艾雷斯比
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Skipwith於大約1360年出生在英國林可爾史爾時，她的父親William Skipwith II37歲，母親Alice Hiltoft35歲。她於大約1377年與Alexander Surtees結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1400年1月1日之後。

nameMeanings.name: Skipwith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: History: The main Skipwith family held the manor of Skipwith in England in the early Middle Ages, and direct descendants can be traced to the present day. In the 13th century they moved from Yorkshire to Lincolnshire, where their principal seat was at southern Ormsby. In the early 17th century there was further migration, to Leicestershire, Warwickshire, and across the Atlantic to VA. Other bearers of the name seem to have been tenants of Lincolnshire manors held by the Skipworth family, and to have taken the surname of their overlords.

variants.name: Skipworth
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): habitational name from Skipwith (East Yorkshire), from Old Northumbrian scīp (Old English scēap ‘sheep’), + Old English wīc ‘dwelling, specialized farm’, later replaced by Old Norse vithr ‘wood’; initial Sk-, instead of Sh-, is also due to Scandinavian influence.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Skipwith was born about 1360, in Lincolnshire, England, her father, William Skipwith II, was 37 and her mother, Alice Hiltoft, was 35. She married Alexander Surtees about 1377. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died after 1 January 1400.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Skipwith(이)가 대략 1360년 영국 링코실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Skipwith II(은)는 37세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Hiltoft(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 대략 1377년 Alexander Surtees(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1400년 1월 1일 이후 사망했다.
